perturbation.original_text: The term "Specified Minimum Percentage" shall be equal to the percentage set forth on Schedule 1 attached hereto, which shall be agreed to by AIG and the Company at least once every year beginning upon the date of the filing of the Company's 2014 Annual Statement with the Domiciliary State's insurance department and following review against the capital adequacy standards and criteria ("Agency Criteria") of each of Standard & Poor's Corp. ("S&P"), Moody's Investors Service ("Moody's") and A.M. Best Company ("A.M. Best").
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Specified Minimum Percentage" shall be equal to the percentage set forth on Schedule 1 attached hereto, which shall be determined solely by AIG and may be revised at AIG's discretion without requiring agreement from the Company. This percentage is subject to change at any time during the year.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in how the 'Specified Minimum Percentage' is determined. Originally, it required agreement between AIG and the Company annually. Now, it states AIG determines it alone and may change it anytime. This contradicts section 5, where the company agrees to comply with all financial planning requirements set forth by AIG, which creates uncertainty about whether the Company has any real control over essential financial metrics.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the requirements of applicable law and the approval, to the extent required, by any or all of the Company's senior management, relevant management committees, board of directors, and of any insurance regulator, the Company hereby acknowledges that, in a manner consistent with past practice and any other reasonable requirements of AIG, it will comply with all financial and budgetary planning, risk mitigation, derisking or pricing, corporate governance, investment, informational and procedural requirements set forth by AIG.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the process for determining the 'Specified Minimum Percentage.' Initially, it required mutual agreement between AIG and the Company. However, the modified text grants AIG the sole discretion to determine and revise this percentage without the Company's consent. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Company will comply with AIG's financial requirements while also noting it is 'Subject to the requirements of applicable law and the approval, to the extent required, by any or all of the Company's senior management...' The modification removes the Company's control over a key financial metric, thus creating an in-text contradiction regarding the extent of the company's autonomy in financial planning as outlined in the contradicted text. The new text creates uncertainty regarding whether the company has any say in financial metrics.

perturbation.original_text: AIG shall have the absolute right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the Company, which notice shall state the effective date of termination (the "Termination Date"); provided, however, that AIG agrees not to terminate this Agreement unless (a) AIG significantly modifies the 3 corporate structure or ownership of the Company, or (b) AIG sells the Company to an acquirer, in each case, (i) having a rating from at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, that is at least equal to the lower of (x) AIG's then-current rating from such agency or (y) the Company's then-current rating as supported by this Agreement from such agency; or (ii) such that, immediately on the effective date of the modification of corporate structure or sale by AIG of the Company, the Company's capitalization is consistent with the minimum capital adequacy standards and criteria of at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization, for a rating that is equal to or better than the Company's then-current rating on the date immediately preceding such modification of corporate structure or sale.
perturbation.changed_text: AIG shall have the absolute right to terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to the Company, which notice shall state the effective date of termination (the "Termination Date"); provided, however, that AIG agrees to use its best efforts not to terminate this Agreement unless (a) AIG significantly modifies the 3 corporate structure or ownership of the Company, or (b) AIG sells the Company to an acquirer, in each case, (i) having a rating from at least one of S&P, Moody's, A.M. Best or a substitute agency, which is a nationally recognized statistical rating organization. The conditions provided in (i) and (ii) are purely for AIG's internal assessment and are not binding obligations.
perturbation.explanation: The modification of 'agrees not to terminate' to 'agrees to use its best efforts not to terminate', and the added sentence that 'The conditions provided in (i) and (ii) are purely for AIG's internal assessment and are not binding obligations', introduces a contradiction. The original text creates a condition for termination related to ratings. Now it suggests such conditions are non-binding. This creates uncertainty for policyholders, whose rights are discussed in Section 8, about whether the protection afforded by the Agreement's continuation is real or illusory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any policyholder holding a policy issued by the Company prior to the termination of this Agreement shall have the right to demand that the Company enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 3 and 4 of this Agreement, and, if the Company fails or refuses to take timely action to enforce such rights or the Company defaults in any claim or other payment owed to any such policyholder when due, such policyholder may proceed directly against AIG to enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 3 and 4 of this Agreement; provided, however, that no policyholder of the Company may take any action authorized under this paragraph 8 unless and until (a) such policyholder has given AIG written notice of its intent to enforce the terms of this Agreement as provided in this paragraph 8, which notice shall specify in reasonable detail the nature of and basis for the policyholder's complaint and (b) AIG has failed to comply with this Agreement within sixty (60) days after such notice is given; and, provided, further, that upon termination of this Agreement in accordance with paragraph 7 hereof, the rights of any policyholder as provided for under this paragraph 8 shall terminate effective as of the Termination Date, except with respect to the obligation of AIG (if any) to make capital contributions to the Company pursuant to paragraphs 1, 3 and 4 of this Agreement solely to the extent such obligation arose prior to, and remained unsatisfied as of, the Termination Date (it being understood that upon AIG's satisfaction of all such obligations after the Termination Date, no such policyholder shall have any rights against the Company or AIG, as the case may be, under this paragraph 8).
perturbation.justification: The original text provides AIG with the right to terminate the agreement under specific conditions (ratings, capital adequacy). The change modifies 'agrees not to terminate' to 'agrees to use its best efforts not to terminate', and it explicitly states that those conditions are 'purely for AIG's internal assessment and are not binding obligations'. This directly contradicts Section 8, which grants policyholders the right to enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs (which include the original termination conditions). The modification turns the termination conditions into non-binding suggestions, undermining the enforceability of the agreement as it relates to policyholder rights as defined in Section 8. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of Ambiguities.

perturbation.original_text: AIG hereby waives any failure or delay on the part of the Company in asserting or enforcing any of its rights or in making any claims or demands hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: AIG hereby waives any failure or delay on the part of the Company in asserting or enforcing any of its rights or in making any claims or demands hereunder, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct by the Company, as determined solely by AIG.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the waiver, but leaves 'gross negligence or willful misconduct' undefined, and gives AIG sole discretion in determining its existence. This contradicts the initial waiver, creating an in-text ambiguity. This is contradictory with Section 6, where the initial text AIG waives any failure or delay on the part of the company. The perturbed statement now adds a new exception which is being defined soley by AIG.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AIG hereby waives any failure or delay on the part of the Company in asserting or enforcing any of its rights or in making any claims or demands hereunder, except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct by the Company, as determined solely by AIG.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('except in cases of gross negligence or willful misconduct by the Company, as determined solely by AIG') to the initial waiver provided in both the original and contradicted texts. This exception, coupled with AIG's sole discretion in determining its existence, creates an ambiguity and potential conflict. The initial waiver implies a comprehensive relinquishment of AIG's right to claim failure or delay, but the exception allows AIG to selectively invoke these rights based on its own determination, thus contradicting the scope of the original waiver. This is because the meaning of gross negligence or willful misconduct is not defined, and since AIG will determine it, there is no true or consistent basis for the waiver.
